page: Amazon Web Services r6id.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance includes 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The r6id.metal achieves top-tier status in PassMark CPU tests, including the overall CPU score of 88,191.68, extended instructions, and encryption tests. Multi-core performance is further validated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 124,966.04 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17,782.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 338.87 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 132.33 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database and web serving capabilities are also robust, demonstrated by 110.69 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 74.66 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6id.metal offers a high-density resource profile by combining massive memory capacity with fast local NVMe storage on a bare-metal platform, eliminating virtualization overhead. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,670.0, indicating that workloads relying heavily on single-threaded execution may not fully exploit this hardware. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, Redis caching layers, real-time data processing, and enterprise applications requiring direct hardware access.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server configured with 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 50 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for PassMark CPU, encryption, and extended instruction tests, alongside strong multi-core execution and high memory bandwidth of up to 338.87 GB/sec for large block reads. While single-core performance is average, its massive memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for resource-dense workloads. The r6id.metal is designed for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and enterprise applications requiring direct physical hardware access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and strong multi-core performance for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core CPU, encryption, and database benchmark performance
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and bare-metal workloads
